lesson.title: how to *fully develop* body paragraphs - writing task 2 double question/two-part question essay

videos.title: how to *fully develop* body paragraphs - writing task 2 double question/two-part question essay

vocabularies.definition: To elaborate on an idea or topic by providing details and explanations.
examples: The writer needs to develop their argument further.
The company is developing a new product.
vocabularies.contextSentence: The focus of this video will be on how to develop your body paragraphs.

vocabularies.definition: An IELTS Writing Task 2 question that asks two separate questions within the same prompt.
examples: The essay requires you to address a double question.
Double questions often require two body paragraphs.
vocabularies.contextSentence: about how to answer double questions, also known as two-part questions.

vocabularies.definition: A set of rules or instructions that a computer follows to solve a problem or complete a task.
examples: Search engine algorithms determine the order of search results.
Social media algorithms curate content based on user preferences.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Social media algorithms are extremely good at providing people with content that will hold their attention.

vocabularies.definition: To keep someone interested in something.
examples: The speaker struggled to hold the audience's attention.
The colorful graphics helped to hold the viewers' attention.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Social media algorithms are extremely good at providing people with content that will hold their attention.

vocabularies.definition: The level of interaction and involvement a user has with a platform or content.
examples: The company is trying to increase user engagement.
High engagement rates indicate that the content is popular.
vocabularies.contextSentence: They also collect data on user engagement so that they can serve up content that is more likely to keep users on the platforms for longer.

vocabularies.definition: The ability to control one's impulses and behaviors.
examples: Children often struggle with self-control.
Developing self-control is important for academic success.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Since children tend to lack self-control, they may be unable to resist those personalized posts and wind up wasting too much time on their phones.

vocabularies.definition: The process of developing or moving forward in a logical and organized way.
examples: The essay lacked a clear progression of ideas.
The story followed a logical progression.
vocabularies.translation: sự tiến triển, sự phát triển
vocabularies.contextSentence: Now I’m gonna show you how to turn a list of items into good progression.

vocabularies.definition: Causing harm or damage, often used to describe negative or harmful content.
examples: The online comments were often toxic.
The company is trying to remove toxic content from its platform.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Because many social media posts are toxic, consuming them may make children unhappy with who they are.

vocabularies.definition: Not representing things as they really are; not practical or achievable.
examples: The expectations were unrealistic.
The movie portrayed an unrealistic view of life.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Perfectly filtered pictures and staged videos on social media create an unrealistic world for children scrolling through their feeds.

vocabularies.definition: To state or describe something clearly and precisely.
examples: The instructions specify the required materials.
The report should specify the findings in detail.
vocabularies.contextSentence: In our example, we can specify exactly what they are unhappy about.

vocabularies.definition: A young person who is developing into an adult.
examples: Adolescent girls are particularly vulnerable to body image issues.
The study focused on adolescent behavior.
vocabularies.contextSentence: After seeing post after post of fitness influencers who appear to have perfect lives and bodies, some adolescent girls may find it very difficult to refrain from comparing themselves to those influencers.

vocabularies.definition: To stop oneself from doing something.
examples: Please refrain from smoking in this area.
She refrained from commenting on the situation.
vocabularies.contextSentence: After seeing post after post of fitness influencers who appear to have perfect lives and bodies, some adolescent girls may find it very difficult to refrain from comparing themselves to those influencers.

vocabularies.definition: To have a negative effect on someone or something.
examples: Stress can take a toll on your health.
The economic downturn took a toll on the company's profits.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Sadly, this may take a toll on their mental health.

grammars.title: Using 'Why is this the case?'
grammars.explanation: The video explains that when the question uses the plural 'reasons' and asks 'what are the reasons?', you need to write more than one reason. However, when the question is 'why is this the case?', you can give one or two reasons.
examples: Why is this the case?
What are the reasons?
grammars.contextSentence: So if they use the plural “reasons” and ask “what are the reasons?”, then we have to write more than one reason.

grammars.title: Using 'rather than'
grammars.explanation: This phrase is used to show contrast or to indicate a preference for one thing over another.
examples: She chose to study history rather than science.
He preferred reading rather than watching TV.
grammars.contextSentence: This is listing items rather than developing the main topic.

grammars.title: Using 'like' as an example
grammars.explanation: The word 'like' is used to introduce examples or to clarify a general statement.
examples: I enjoy outdoor activities, like hiking and camping.
She likes to eat fruits like apples and oranges.
grammars.contextSentence: So we can change it to: they track user behavior like search history and engagement.

grammars.title: Using 'Since' to introduce a reason
grammars.explanation: The word 'since' is used to introduce a reason or cause.
examples: Since it was raining, we stayed indoors.
Since I was tired, I went to bed early.
grammars.contextSentence: Since children tend to lack self-control, they may be unable to resist those personalized posts and wind up wasting too much time on their phones.

grammars.title: Using 'may' for possibility
grammars.explanation: The modal verb 'may' is used to express possibility or probability.
examples: It may rain later today.
She may decide to go to the party.
grammars.contextSentence: Since children tend to lack self-control, they may be unable to resist those personalized posts and wind up wasting too much time on their phones.

grammars.title: Using 'in order to'
grammars.explanation: This phrase is used to express the purpose or reason for doing something.
examples: He studied hard in order to pass the exam.
They saved money in order to buy a new car.
grammars.contextSentence: Again, in order for the paragraph to have only one topic, it’s best to choose only one side to discuss.

grammars.title: Using 'because' to introduce a reason
grammars.explanation: The word 'because' is used to introduce a reason or cause.
examples: I stayed home because I was sick.
She was happy because she got a good grade.
grammars.contextSentence: Because many social media posts are toxic, consuming them may make children unhappy with who they are.

lesson.notesContent:
# Children — Ghi chú học tập (Band 6.0)

## Từ vựng quan trọng

### develop `/dɪˈveləp/`
**Nghĩa:** To elaborate on an idea or topic by providing details and explanations.
**Dịch:** phát triển, triển khai
**Ví dụ:** *The writer needs to develop their argument further.*

### double question `/ˈdʌbəl ˈkwɛstʃən/`
**Nghĩa:** An IELTS Writing Task 2 question that asks two separate questions within the same prompt.
**Dịch:** câu hỏi kép
**Ví dụ:** *The essay requires you to address a double question.*

### algorithm `/ˈælɡərɪðəm/`
**Nghĩa:** A set of rules or instructions that a computer follows to solve a problem or complete a task.
**Dịch:** thuật toán
**Ví dụ:** *Search engine algorithms determine the order of search results.*

### hold attention `/hoʊld əˈtɛnʃən/`
**Nghĩa:** To keep someone interested in something.
**Dịch:** giữ sự chú ý
**Ví dụ:** *The speaker struggled to hold the audience's attention.*

### engagement `/ɪnˈɡeɪdʒmənt/`
**Nghĩa:** The level of interaction and involvement a user has with a platform or content.
**Dịch:** sự tương tác
**Ví dụ:** *The company is trying to increase user engagement.*

### self-control `/ˌself kənˈtroʊl/`
**Nghĩa:** The ability to control one's impulses and behaviors.
**Dịch:** khả năng tự kiểm soát
**Ví dụ:** *Children often struggle with self-control.*

### progression `/prəˈɡreʃən/`
**Nghĩa:** The process of developing or moving forward in a logical and organized way.
**Dịch:** sự tiến triển, sự phát triển
**Ví dụ:** *The essay lacked a clear progression of ideas.*

### toxic `/ˈtɒksɪk/`
**Nghĩa:** Causing harm or damage, often used to describe negative or harmful content.
**Dịch:** độc hại
**Ví dụ:** *The online comments were often toxic.*

### unrealistic `/ˌʌnrɪəˈlɪstɪk/`
**Nghĩa:** Not representing things as they really are; not practical or achievable.
**Dịch:** phi thực tế
**Ví dụ:** *The expectations were unrealistic.*

### specify `/ˈspɛsɪfaɪ/`
**Nghĩa:** To state or describe something clearly and precisely.
**Dịch:** chỉ rõ
**Ví dụ:** *The instructions specify the required materials.*

### adolescent `/ˌædəˈlɛsənt/`
**Nghĩa:** A young person who is developing into an adult.
**Dịch:** thanh thiếu niên
**Ví dụ:** *Adolescent girls are particularly vulnerable to body image issues.*

### refrain from `/rɪˈfreɪn frɒm/`
**Nghĩa:** To stop oneself from doing something.
**Dịch:** kiềm chế, tránh
**Ví dụ:** *Please refrain from smoking in this area.*

## Điểm ngữ pháp

### Using 'Why is this the case?'
**Cấu trúc:** `Question structure`
The video explains that when the question uses the plural 'reasons' and asks 'what are the reasons?', you need to write more than one reason. However, when the question is 'why is this the case?', you can give one or two reasons.
> Why is this the case?

### Using 'rather than'
**Cấu trúc:** `Verb + rather than + noun/gerund`
This phrase is used to show contrast or to indicate a preference for one thing over another.
> She chose to study history rather than science.

### Using 'like' as an example
**Cấu trúc:** `General statement + like + specific examples`
The word 'like' is used to introduce examples or to clarify a general statement.
> I enjoy outdoor activities, like hiking and camping.

### Using 'Since' to introduce a reason
**Cấu trúc:** `Since + clause, clause`
The word 'since' is used to introduce a reason or cause.
> Since it was raining, we stayed indoors.

### Using 'may' for possibility
**Cấu trúc:** `Subject + may + base verb`
The modal verb 'may' is used to express possibility or probability.
> It may rain later today.

### Using 'in order to'
**Cấu trúc:** `In order to + base verb`
This phrase is used to express the purpose or reason for doing something.
> He studied hard in order to pass the exam.

lesson.title: Discuss both views: Children learning to be good members of society

videos.title: Discuss both views: Children learning to be good members of society

vocabularies.definition: a discussion involving different points of view; a formal discussion on a particular topic in a meeting or legislative assembly, in which opposing arguments are put forward
examples: The issue has been the subject of much debate.
The debate over the new policy is ongoing.
vocabularies.contextSentence: the topic of who should be teaching children how to be good members of society has become a matter of increasing debate in these times!

vocabularies.definition: expressing the meaning of something using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity.
examples: The student was penalized for excessive paraphrasing.
Can you paraphrase this sentence for me?
vocabularies.contextSentence: However, don't take your paraphrasing to the point where you say that a few individuals and some people mean the same thing because they don't

vocabularies.definition: the word 'the' used before a noun to indicate that the identity of the noun is known to the speaker.
examples: The definitive article is essential for clarity.
He forgot to use the definitive article in his essay.
vocabularies.contextSentence: here here here we should not use the definitive article before a plural noun when speaking in general.

vocabularies.definition: the use of successive verbal constructions in poetry or prose that correspond in grammatical structure, sound, meter, meaning, etc.
examples: The essay lacked parallelism, making it difficult to read.
Parallelism improves the flow and readability of the text.
vocabularies.contextSentence: and this is an advanced grammar structure called parallelism.

vocabularies.definition: a unit of grammatical organization next below the sentence in rank and in traditional grammar said to consist of a subject and predicate.
examples: The sentence contains two clauses.
The first clause is the main clause.
vocabularies.contextSentence: when you have a sentence like this where you have two clauses, such responsibility lies on the parents shoulders, so here we have such responsibility begins the

vocabularies.definition: enough or more than enough; plentiful.
examples: The school provides ample opportunities for students.
There is ample evidence to support the claim.
vocabularies.contextSentence: this provides schools with ample opportunity to teach young minds about the basis.

vocabularies.definition: a particular area or neighborhood.
examples: The school is located in my locality.
The locality is known for its friendly atmosphere.
vocabularies.contextSentence: for instance many of the schools in my locality have special classes that teach students about rules

vocabularies.definition: the customary code of polite behavior in society or among members of a particular group.
examples: Good etiquette is essential in formal settings.
She was praised for her excellent etiquette.
vocabularies.contextSentence: could you explain to me the difference between manners and etiquette?

vocabularies.definition: schools, colleges, and universities.
examples: Academic institutions play a crucial role in society.
He is a professor at a prestigious academic institution.
vocabularies.contextSentence: in everyday life hence it is the view of many that academic institutions and you

vocabularies.definition: easily influenced because of a lack of critical ability.
examples: Children are at their most impressionable during their early years.
She is at an impressionable age.
vocabularies.contextSentence: during the most impressionable times

vocabularies.definition: gradually but firmly establish (an idea or attitude) in a person's mind.
examples: The teachers instilled a love of learning in their students.
The parents instilled a sense of responsibility in their children.
vocabularies.contextSentence: therefore if the parents can effectively instill a sense of good character now

vocabularies.definition: expressing a condition.
examples: This is a first conditional sentence.
The essay uses zero and first conditionals.
vocabularies.contextSentence: zero or first conditional this idea what idea is okay we have another

vocabularies.definition: with the possibility to happen or be true.
examples: The new policy could potentially improve the situation.
The project is potentially very successful.
vocabularies.contextSentence: although schools can potentially shape the minds of many young children, their

vocabularies.definition: statements that show a difference between two things.
examples: The essay uses contrasting statements to present both sides of the argument.
The author uses contrasting statements to highlight the differences.
vocabularies.contextSentence: A couple days ago, she wrote about how the imperfect use of contrasting statements.

grammars.title: Using 'some' vs. 'a few'
grammars.explanation: The word 'some' is less specific than 'a few'. 'A few' implies a small number, while 'some' is more general.
examples: Some people believe...
A few students attended the lecture.
grammars.contextSentence: However, don't take your paraphrasing to the point where you say that a few individuals and some people mean the same thing because they don't

grammars.title: Using the definite article 'the' with plural nouns
grammars.explanation: Do not use 'the' before a plural noun when speaking in general terms.
examples: The students are studying (specific students).
Students study (in general).
grammars.structure: The + plural noun (incorrect in general statements)
grammars.contextSentence: here here here we should not use the definitive article before a plural noun when speaking in general.

grammars.title: Parallel Structure
grammars.explanation: Use the same grammatical structure when listing similar ideas or arguments to improve readability and clarity.
examples: She likes to read books, whereas he prefers to watch movies.
The company focuses on innovation, while the competitor emphasizes cost-cutting.
grammars.contextSentence: whereas others argue this should be taught by schools.

grammars.title: Missing commas
grammars.explanation: Commas are used to separate items in a list, and to separate clauses.
examples: I bought apples, bananas, and oranges.
I went to the store, but it was closed.
grammars.contextSentence: once again missing comma, they spend a quarter of their day that would be six

grammars.title: Using 'of' with plural nouns
grammars.explanation: The structure 'of + plural noun' is often incorrect.
examples: The children's lives (correct)
Of children's lives (incorrect)
grammars.contextSentence: okay hopefully I don't have to explain why of children the schools are a and then definitely

grammars.title: Using 'when' with time
grammars.explanation: Use 'when' to introduce a clause about time.
examples: When I was young, I loved to play.
When the time comes, we will act.
grammars.contextSentence: when with with time we use when when character development is at a vital stage and... instead of developing this

grammars.title: First Conditional
grammars.explanation: Used to talk about a possible situation and its likely result.
examples: If it rains, I will stay inside.
If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
grammars.structure: If + present simple, will + base form
grammars.contextSentence: therefore if the parents can effectively instill a sense of good character now

lesson.notesContent:
# Children — Ghi chú học tập (Band 6.0)

## Từ vựng quan trọng

### debate `/dɪˈbeɪt/`
**Nghĩa:** a discussion involving different points of view; a formal discussion on a particular topic in a meeting or legislative assembly, in which opposing arguments are put forward
**Dịch:** tranh luận
**Ví dụ:** *The issue has been the subject of much debate.*

### paraphrasing `/ˈpærəfreɪzɪŋ/`
**Nghĩa:** expressing the meaning of something using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity.
**Dịch:** diễn giải
**Ví dụ:** *The student was penalized for excessive paraphrasing.*

### definitive article `/dɪˈfɪnətɪv ˈɑːrtɪkl/`
**Nghĩa:** the word 'the' used before a noun to indicate that the identity of the noun is known to the speaker.
**Dịch:** mạo từ xác định
**Ví dụ:** *The definitive article is essential for clarity.*

### parallelism `/ˈpærəlelɪzəm/`
**Nghĩa:** the use of successive verbal constructions in poetry or prose that correspond in grammatical structure, sound, meter, meaning, etc.
**Dịch:** tính song song
**Ví dụ:** *The essay lacked parallelism, making it difficult to read.*

### clauses `/klɔːz/`
**Nghĩa:** a unit of grammatical organization next below the sentence in rank and in traditional grammar said to consist of a subject and predicate.
**Dịch:** mệnh đề
**Ví dụ:** *The sentence contains two clauses.*

### ample `/ˈæmpl/`
**Nghĩa:** enough or more than enough; plentiful.
**Dịch:** đủ, nhiều
**Ví dụ:** *The school provides ample opportunities for students.*

### locality `/ləˈkæləti/`
**Nghĩa:** a particular area or neighborhood.
**Dịch:** địa phương
**Ví dụ:** *The school is located in my locality.*

### etiquette `/ˈetɪket/`
**Nghĩa:** the customary code of polite behavior in society or among members of a particular group.
**Dịch:** phép xã giao
**Ví dụ:** *Good etiquette is essential in formal settings.*

### academic institutions `/ˌækəˈdemɪk ˌɪnstɪˈtjuːʃənz/`
**Nghĩa:** schools, colleges, and universities.
**Dịch:** các tổ chức học thuật
**Ví dụ:** *Academic institutions play a crucial role in society.*

### impressionable `/ɪmˈpreʃənəbl/`
**Nghĩa:** easily influenced because of a lack of critical ability.
**Dịch:** dễ bị ảnh hưởng
**Ví dụ:** *Children are at their most impressionable during their early years.*

### instill `/ɪnˈstɪl/`
**Nghĩa:** gradually but firmly establish (an idea or attitude) in a person's mind.
**Dịch:** truyền đạt
**Ví dụ:** *The teachers instilled a love of learning in their students.*

### conditional `/kənˈdɪʃənəl/`
**Nghĩa:** expressing a condition.
**Dịch:** câu điều kiện
**Ví dụ:** *This is a first conditional sentence.*

## Điểm ngữ pháp

### Using 'some' vs. 'a few'
**Cấu trúc:** `some + plural noun`
The word 'some' is less specific than 'a few'. 'A few' implies a small number, while 'some' is more general.
> Some people believe...

### Using the definite article 'the' with plural nouns
**Cấu trúc:** `The + plural noun (incorrect in general statements)`
Do not use 'the' before a plural noun when speaking in general terms.
> The students are studying (specific students).

### Parallel Structure
**Cấu trúc:** `Clause 1, whereas clause 2`
Use the same grammatical structure when listing similar ideas or arguments to improve readability and clarity.
> She likes to read books, whereas he prefers to watch movies.

### Missing commas
**Cấu trúc:** `Item 1, item 2, and item 3.`
Commas are used to separate items in a list, and to separate clauses.
> I bought apples, bananas, and oranges.

### Using 'of' with plural nouns
**Cấu trúc:** `of + plural noun (incorrect)`
The structure 'of + plural noun' is often incorrect.
> The children's lives (correct)

### Using 'when' with time
**Cấu trúc:** `When + clause`
Use 'when' to introduce a clause about time.
> When I was young, I loved to play.

Phát âm: /dɪˈbeɪt/
Ví dụ: The issue has been the subject of much debate.

Nghĩa: diễn giải
Phát âm: /ˈpærəfreɪzɪŋ/
Ví dụ: The student was penalized for excessive paraphrasing.

Nghĩa: mạo từ xác định
Phát âm: /dɪˈfɪnətɪv ˈɑːrtɪkl/
Ví dụ: The definitive article is essential for clarity.

Nghĩa: tính song song
Phát âm: /ˈpærəlelɪzəm/
Ví dụ: The essay lacked parallelism, making it difficult to read.

Nghĩa: mệnh đề
Phát âm: /klɔːz/
Ví dụ: The sentence contains two clauses.

Nghĩa: đủ, nhiều
Phát âm: /ˈæmpl/
Ví dụ: The school provides ample opportunities for students.
Đồng nghĩa: abundant, bountiful, capacious, copious

Nghĩa: địa phương
Phát âm: /ləˈkæləti/
Ví dụ: The school is located in my locality.

Nghĩa: phép xã giao
Phát âm: /ˈetɪket/
Ví dụ: Good etiquette is essential in formal settings.

Nghĩa: các tổ chức học thuật
Phát âm: /ˌækəˈdemɪk ˌɪnstɪˈtjuːʃənz/
Ví dụ: Academic institutions play a crucial role in society.

Nghĩa: dễ bị ảnh hưởng
Phát âm: /ɪmˈpreʃənəbl/
Ví dụ: Children are at their most impressionable during their early years.

Nghĩa: truyền đạt
Phát âm: /ɪnˈstɪl/
Ví dụ: The teachers instilled a love of learning in their students.

Nghĩa: câu điều kiện
Phát âm: /kənˈdɪʃənəl/
Ví dụ: This is a first conditional sentence.
Đồng nghĩa: if-then, material conditional, hypothetical, conditioned

Nghĩa: có khả năng
Phát âm: /pəˈtenʃəli/
